name: slice_gap
description: Slice tool 'Gap' + 'Offset Side' options (mesh.sliceTool, task 0275 S9). A cube (8v/6f) cut by the plane through the drawn Start->End line perpendicular to the work plane (headless work-plane normal = default world XZ, +Y). The line runs along Z through the origin, so the cut-plane normal n = normalize(cross(end-start, +Y)) = -X (through X=0), the == S0 slice.json midX cut. Split is ON in every case (S7): each of the 4 crossing verts at x=0 is DUPLICATED into a lo/hi pair, splitting the cube into two disconnected shells along the cut (16v/24e). Cap Sections is ON (S8): each shell's boundary loop is sealed by one cap polygon (16v/24e/12f, no new verts/edges). GAP (S9) then pushes the two shells APART along the plane normal n by exactly `gap`, offset per `gapSide` -- POSITIONS ONLY (topology identical to the gap=0 caps-on case: 16v/24e/12f throughout), so the caps gain real (nonzero) area, becoming solid walls of the opened band. WHICH SHELL IS WHICH: `lo` (the ORIGINAL crossing verts 8-11) is kept by the +n-side faces, so it is the +n shell (here +n = -X => the x<0 shell); `hi` (the DUPLICATES 12-15) is the -n shell (x>0). Opening the shells apart moves lo toward +n (-X) and hi toward -n (+X). OFFSET SIDE (total shell separation is always exactly `gap`): center = symmetric (lo +n*gap/2, hi -n*gap/2); positive = the +n shell (lo) takes the FULL gap along +n, hi stays on the plane; negative = the -n shell (hi) takes the FULL gap along -n, lo stays on the plane. gap=0 leaves every pair coincident (byte-for-byte S7/S8). KEY DIVERGENCE from the Loop Slice gap, which pushes seam pairs along the on-surface RAIL (perpendicular to the edge loop): a FLAT plane cut has no rail, so the two shells separate along the plane normal instead. The kernel-level topology + exact separation proof lives in the source/mesh.d cutByPlaneEx S9 unittest.
source: Analytic. Cube +/-0.5; plane law = math.planeForSlice(start, end, +Y). Line along Z -> n = -X through X=0. Split ON duplicates each crossing vert (dups 12-15 pair with originals 8-11 in order); Cap Sections ON seals each shell; Gap ON displaces lo/hi along n. gap = 0.2 here: center -> lo x=-0.1 / hi x=+0.1; positive -> lo x=-0.2 / hi x=0; negative -> lo x=0 / hi x=+0.2. y,z unchanged (displacement is purely along the plane normal). Gap geometry law is a pure position pass by construction (Mesh.splitAlongCutLoop, task 0275), no reference-app numeric parity needed.
